item.title: NASA’s ESCAPADE Snaps Family Portrait of Earth, Moon
item.description: On July 3, 2026, NASA’s Mars-destined ESCAPADE spacecraft captured photos of Earth and the Moon in visible and thermal infrared light.

item.title: New Small Spacecraft Technology Demonstration Mission at the Moon
item.description: NASA is working with industry to advance the next phase of cislunar infrastructure for the agency’s Artemis program and Moon Base, including orbital assets

item.title: HWO SIG Seminar, 12 August 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: Habitable Worlds Observatory SIG Seminar. Details TBA

item.title: Community Science (Ad ASTRA) Workshop, Sept 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: 1-3 September 2026. The Community Science (Ad ASTRA) Workshop is organized by the NASA Astrophysics Division to engage the community in defining future large, strategic astrophysics missions.

item.title: MCMC Model Visualizer - NASA
item.description: The Mars Climate Modeling Center (MCMC) presents the model visualization tool, a website for interactive plotting and downloading Mars Global Climate Model (MGCM) output.

item.title: Meet NASA's New X-59 Test Pilot - NASA
item.description: NASA’s X-59 experimental aircraft reached a major milestone Friday, June 5, when it flew faster than the speed of

item.title: Michael T. Lippert - NASA
item.description: Michael T. Lippert is a research test pilot at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California. He is assigned to fly the agency’s X-59

item.title: New Crew Members Welcomed to International Space Station - NASA
item.description: From left, Expedition 74 flight engineers Anna Kikina and Pyotr Dubrov of Roscosmos, and Anil Menon of NASA pose for a July 18, 2026, photo while holding a

item.title: NASA to Support Blue Origin New Glenn Rocket Testing, Advance Artemis - NASA
item.description: NASA and Blue Origin announced Friday they recently agreed to conduct second stage hot fire testing for the company’s New Glenn rocket on the B-2 test stand

item.title: Crew Works on Biomedicine, Spacesuits Day Before Swapping Commanders - NASA
item.description: The Expedition 74 crew is wrapping up its final weekend together with biomedical checks, spacesuit cleaning, and cargo packing inside the Soyuz MS-28 crew spacecraft. The orbital residents will swap commanders on Saturday the day before three crewmates depart the International Space Station for a ride back to Earth.
